items.q: What is the capacity of short-term memory according to Miller?
items.why: Students confuse capacity (items) with duration (about 18-30 seconds without rehearsal). Miller's magic number describes how many CHUNKS, not how long.

items.q: What are the three elements of Beck's negative triad?
items.correct: Negative views of self, world, and future.
items.misconception: Negative views of past, present, and future.
items.why: Students often substitute 'past' for 'self' or 'world'. The triad is about the DOMAINS of negative thought (self/world/future), not time periods.

items.q: Which component handles visual and spatial information?
items.why: Students mix up the slave systems. The phonological loop is for auditory/verbal information; the sketchpad is for visual/spatial. The central executive coordinates but doesn't store.

items.q: In Loftus & Palmer 1974, which verb produced the highest mean speed estimate?
items.why: Correct answer: Smashed. “Hit” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which is NOT one of Geiselman's four Cognitive Interview techniques?
items.why: It's Hypnotise witness. “Report everything” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what year did Loftus and Palmer publish their verb intensity study?
items.why: The real figure is 1974. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
